name: Stinging Nettle Root Extract
historicalUse: Stinging nettle is one of the oldest and most universally employed medicinal plants. Dioscorides described urtica for joint pain, kidney stones, and urinary conditions. Ibn Sina included it in the Canon as a warming, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory plant. Medieval European monastics used it extensively for urinary and joint conditions. The root specifically (versus the leaf) was used in Arabic and European traditions for male urinary tract support.
modernEvidence: Nettle root extract inhibits sex hormone binding globulin (SHBG), reducing SHBG-testosterone binding and increasing free testosterone bioavailability. It also inhibits aromatase (reducing estrogen synthesis from androgens) and has been shown to reduce prostate epithelial cell proliferation in vitro. Multiple German clinical trials support its use in BPH, and Commission E has approved nettle root for irritable bladder and BPH symptoms.
mechanisms: SHBG inhibition — nettle root lectins bind SHBG, reducing testosterone binding and increasing free androgen availability
Aromatase inhibition — reduces peripheral conversion of androgens to estrogens
Anti-proliferative — inhibits EGFR and TGF-beta signaling in prostate epithelium
5-LOX inhibition — anti-inflammatory in prostate and urinary tract tissue
Aquaretic — mild increase in urinary volume without electrolyte disturbance
benefits: Supports healthy urinary flow and prostate function
Promotes normal urinary frequency and comfort
Contributes to prostate tissue inflammatory balance
safetyProfile: Well tolerated. Commission E approved. Mild GI discomfort possible. Distinct from nettle leaf — root extract has different pharmacology and uses.
contraindications: Fluid restriction states — mild diuretic effect
drugInteractions: Antidiabetic drugs — mild glucose-lowering effect; monitor blood glucose
Warfarin — monitor if taking large amounts long-term
physicianNotes: Root-specific extract is required — nettle leaf has different chemistry and indications. Commission E approved for BPH stage I-II. Synergistic with saw palmetto in urinary support protocols (complementary mechanisms). Advise PSA disclosure to physicians as with all BPH-supportive agents.
patientGuidance: This formulation uses nettle root specifically — which has different properties from nettle leaf. Take with food. If you take diuretic medications, inform your physician.
